course_description:
This course details the theoretical and practical considerations required to perform a basic abdomen and pelvis MR examinations with particular reference to the liver imaging, intestines, prostate and female pelvis. Students will be able to recognise normal anatomy and common pathologies, be able to tailor the examination accordingly, and to critically evaluate the final images. Students will also be aware of appropriate patient care and understand appropriate equipment set up and preparation.

This course will provide students with a broad understanding of the use of Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) to image areas of the abdomen and pelvis. This course will include clinical indications, theoretical & practical considerations required to perform basic abdomen and pelvis MR examinations with particular reference to liver imaging, enterography, prostate and female pelvis.

restrictions: Restricted to students enrolled in the Magnetic Resonance Technology program suite.
Another instance of the same course
This course is offered in in-person and external mode.

timetable:
The timetable for this course is available on the

.
course_aims: This course aims to equip students with theoretical and practical considerations required to perform a basic abdomen and Pelvis MR examinations.

learning_outcomes.description: Apply theoretical techniques to perform a basic musculoskeletal MR examination, with particular reference to liver imaging, enterography, prostate and female pelvis.
Distinguish between normal anatomy and pathologies in MR images
Construct and tailor the examination protocol accordingly
Establish appropriate patient care and understand equipment and patient set up

assessment_summary.title: Online Quizes
assessment_summary.due_date: Quiz 1 should be completed prior to: 5/09/2025 2:00 pm; Quiz 2 should be completed prior to: 17/10/2025 2:00 pm; Quiz 1 is available from Friday 29 August 9:00 (week 5).; Quiz 2 is available from Friday 10 October 9:00 (week 10).

assessment_details.title: Online Quizes
assessment_details.due_date: Quiz 1 should be completed prior to: 5/09/2025 2:00 pm Quiz 2 should be completed prior to: 17/10/2025 2:00 pm Quiz 1 is available from Friday 29 August 9:00 (week 5). Quiz 2 is available from Friday 10 October 9:00 (week 10).
assessment_details.task_description: 1) Description Each quiz has 10 Questions for a total of 10 marks. 2) Instructions Quiz questions are True/False, Multiple Choice, Multiple Answer or Short Answer, and are worth marks according to difficulty. Duration The quiz duration is 10 minutes (from the start of the quiz). The quiz can’t be saved. Once the quiz is started, it must to be completed within 10 minutes. The quiz will be submitted automatically once the time expires. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Deferral or extension
You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
An extension is not available for these quizzes due to the answers being discussed in the next available tutorial. However, successful applications for an extension in extenuating circumstances may be offered an alternative assessment. If there are extenuating circumstances why you were unable to complete your quiz then please email smp.student@uq.edu.au. You will need to do this within 5 calendar days of your quiz and include evidence (e.g. medical certificate).
Late submission
You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late .
The quizzes open 7 days before the due date and can not be attempted after the quiz is closed. The quiz can not be attempted after the week window has been closed.

assessment_details.title: Assignment One
assessment_details.task_description: Your paper should aim to be 2000 words (strictly limited to 1800 - 2200 words) Referencing Variation in referencing styles exist in journals relevant to MRI science; therefore, we do not specify a particular style but prefer the use of either the Vancouver or Harvard referencing style. assessment_details.title: End of Semester examination
assessment_details.task_description: The final exam will be an in-person, paper-based exam. UQ Examinations will contact students directly to confirm arrangements, the student will either sit the exam at the St Lucia campus (lives within 200km radius of the campus) or will be directed to nominate an exam centre by a certain date. Please ensure you update your semester address in Si-net at the start of the semester. One reminder will be sent and students who fail to nominate an exam centre and will be automatically assigned to the St Lucia campus. Late nominations will not be accepted. A list of examination centres is listed here, but note that Exam centre availability is subject to change at short notice. Students enrolled internally , please refer to the course profile for Internal students for in-person examination arrangements. Read full task description Hide full task description
